macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Drift Protocol on Solana Suffers $286 Million Exploit via Social Engineering
items.summary: Solana-based Drift Protocol, the largest perpetual futures exchange, lost $286 million in a sophisticated six-month social engineering attack where hackers posed as a quantitative trading firm to breach the security council. The incident highlights ongoing DeFi security risks and has sparked calls for hardware wallet usage.

items.title: Ethereum Finalizes Glamsterdam Upgrade Scope for Early 2026
items.summary: Ethereum developers have finalized the scope for the Glamsterdam hard fork, scheduled for the first half of 2026, aiming to boost scalability and maintain competitiveness against chains like Solana. This upgrade is seen as a game-changer for Ethereum's roadmap.

items.title: Bitcoin Traders Turn Defensive as Put Volume Outpaces Calls 54.87% to 45.13%
items.summary: Bitcoin futures open interest dropped to $46.94B amid contracting positions on major exchanges like Binance and CME, with Deribit put volume significantly outpacing calls, signaling short-term hedging. BTC trades around $66,810-$67,000 with slight dips but institutional support holding steady.

items.title: $129 Million in Crypto Liquidations, Longs Hit Harder at $70M
items.summary: Network-wide liquidations reached $129 million in the past 24 hours, with longs liquidated for $70.25 million versus $47.86 million in shorts; Bitcoin shorts saw $21.7M liquidated while Ethereum longs lost $30.7M. This reflects volatile trading amid market pressure.

items.title: Bitcoin Sentiment Dips to 5-Week Fear Level Amid Price Stall Below $66,800
items.summary: Social sentiment for Bitcoin has hit a 5-week fear low as price action stalls below $66,800, entering the week under doubt despite liquidity spikes and institutional inflows providing some support.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Expands COO Brad Lightcap’s Role as AGI Chief Steps Away on Medical Leave
items.summary: OpenAI is reshuffling leadership with COO Brad Lightcap taking on expanded responsibilities amid the AGI chief's medical leave, as IPO plans loom.

items.title: Anthropic Cuts Claude Subscribers Off from OpenClaw in Cost Crackdown
items.summary: Anthropic requires Claude Code subscribers to pay extra for OpenClaw usage and has restricted access in a move to control costs.

items.title: Microsoft Ships New AI Models, Signals Independence from OpenAI
items.summary: Microsoft launches its own AI models to compete with Google and OpenAI, indicating a push to build an independent AI empire.

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off Entire AI Team Despite Prior Embrace of Technology
items.summary: GTA VI maker Take-Two Interactive has disbanded its entire AI division, including its head, following CEO doubts on AI.

items.title: Meta Freezes AI Data Work After Breach Exposes Training Secrets
items.summary: Meta has halted AI data operations following a security breach that risked exposing proprietary training data.

items.title: Journalist Faces Backlash for Revealing Use of AI in Writing Process
items.summary: A journalist received public criticism after admitting to using AI for research, fact-checking, and data analysis, sparking debate on AI's role in journalism.

items.title: FIU and Baptist Health Develop AI-Powered Digital Stethoscope for Early Heart Disease Detection
items.summary: Researchers combine AI and sound analysis with digital stethoscopes to detect heart disease early, achieving 95% accuracy for healthy sounds and 85% for diseased ones.

items.title: $2B Chasing Anthropic, $600M OpenAI Shares Unsold in Secondary Markets
items.summary: Secondary markets show heavy investment interest in Anthropic with $2B chasing shares, while $600M of OpenAI stock remains unsold, repricing the AI investment race.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung to Discontinue Messages App in US by July 2026
items.summary: Samsung announced it will end support for its Messages app in the US in July 2026, providing instructions for users on older Android versions to switch to Google Messages.

items.title: Planet Labs Withholds Satellite Imagery of Iran on US Government Request
items.summary: US satellite provider Planet Labs is indefinitely restricting access to imagery of Iran and conflict regions following a request from the US government.

items.title: Suspected North Korean Hackers Steal $270M from Drift in Elaborate Scheme
items.summary: Attackers posing as a quant trading firm conducted a 6+ month operation, including in-person meetings and a $1M deposit, to steal $270M from Drift.

items.title: Apple Signs Third-Party Driver for eGPUs on Apple Silicon Macs
items.summary: Apple has approved a Tiny Corp driver for AMD or Nvidia eGPUs on Apple Silicon Macs, targeted at AI research rather than graphics acceleration.
